label: Deepgram
description: Transcribe audio and extract voice intelligence using Deepgram's speech-to-text API.

form.title: Connect Deepgram
form.subtitle: Paste your Deepgram API key to enable speech-to-text and voice intelligence.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste an API key from the Deepgram Console. Fastest setup — no OAuth client required, takes under a minute.
methods.how_to:
## Get a Deepgram API Key

1. Log in at [console.deepgram.com](https://console.deepgram.com).
2. Select or create a **Project** from the top navigation.
3. In the left sidebar click **API Keys**.
4. Click **Create a New API Key**.
5. Give it a name (e.g. `Anton`) and choose a permission level:
   - **Member** — full transcription and voice intelligence access (recommended).
   - **Usage Only** — read usage stats only (too restrictive for most tasks).
6. Click **Create Key**. The secret is shown **once** — copy it immediately.
7. Paste the key into the **API Key** field below.

## Notes

- Keys are project-scoped. If you need access to multiple Deepgram projects, create a separate connector per project.
- To revoke access, return to **API Keys** in the Console and delete the key.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Found in Deepgram Console → your Project → API Keys. Shown only once at creation.

fields.name: base_url
fields.label: API Base URL
fields.description: Override only if you are on a Deepgram self-hosted or on-premises deployment. Leave blank to use the default cloud endpoint.
